questions.text: How should the service extract and validate the bearer token from the Authorization header?
questions.context: The PRD states bearer token auth is required but does not specify parsing rules. For example: is the 'Bearer ' prefix mandatory? Is it case-sensitive? What should happen if the Authorization header is present but malformed (e.g. missing prefix, extra whitespace)?
options: Require exactly 'Bearer <token>' with a capital B and single space; reject anything else with 401
Accept 'Bearer' prefix case-insensitively and trim surrounding whitespace
Treat the entire Authorization header value as the token (no prefix stripping)

questions.text: What HTTP status code should GET /readyz return when the database is unavailable or the readiness check fails?
questions.context: The PRD defines the happy path (200 OK) but not the failure path. Kubernetes readiness probes need a non-2xx status to mark the pod as not ready.
options: 200 OK with a JSON body indicating degraded state

questions.text: What is the Go module path for this service?
questions.context: The project layout references a <module> directory but the module path is needed for go.mod and all internal import paths. This is typically in the form github.com/<org>/<repo>.

questions.text: Which SQLite driver should be used?
questions.context: The PRD says 'an appropriate Go SQLite driver' without specifying one. The two main options have significant implications: mattn/go-sqlite3 requires CGO (complicates cross-compilation and container builds), while modernc.org/sqlite is pure Go (simpler builds, no CGO). This choice affects the Dockerfile and CI pipeline.

questions.text: Should the service handle SIGTERM gracefully (i.e. finish in-flight requests before shutting down)?
questions.context: Graceful shutdown is important for Kubernetes deployments to avoid dropped requests during pod termination. If not specified, the implementer may or may not handle this.
options: Yes — graceful shutdown with a configurable or fixed timeout
Yes — graceful shutdown with a fixed timeout (e.g. 10 seconds)
No — abrupt shutdown is acceptable for this test harness

questions.text: What should the JSON error response body look like for 4xx and 5xx responses?
questions.context: The PRD defers to Echo's default error format, but integration tests may need to assert on the response body. Echo's default is {"message": "..."}. Should the PRD explicitly adopt this format, or define a custom one?
options: No structured body required — status code alone is sufficient for this test harness

questions.text: Are there any testing requirements or expectations for this service (e.g. unit tests, integration tests, minimum coverage)?
questions.context: Since this service is explicitly described as a test harness for agent-fox tooling, it would be useful to clarify whether the service itself must have its own tests and at what level.
options: Unit tests for handler logic only
Integration tests covering the full HTTP-to-SQLite pipeline
No explicit testing requirements for the service itself

answers.q1: Require exactly 'Bearer <token>' with a capital B and single space; reject anything else with 401
answers.q5: Yes — graceful shutdown with a fixed timeout (e.g. 30 seconds)
answers.q7: Integration tests covering the full HTTP-to-SQLite pipeline
